title: Completed orders per customer (including zero)
prompt: Return every customer and their number of **completed** orders — including customers with none (they should show 0, not be dropped). Use a LEFT JOIN. Columns: name, completed_orders. Order by completed_orders descending, then name.

generic: COUNT(CASE WHEN o.status = 'completed' THEN 1 END) AS completed_orders
LEFT JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id

hints: A plain JOIN would drop customers with no orders — use LEFT JOIN to keep them.
Count only completed rows: COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'completed' THEN 1 END).
